title: AGENTS.md
text: projr R package facilitates reproducible archived research projects. primary goal automate full lifecycle research build: rendering documents, bumping versions, committing Git, archiving outputs remote destinations (GitHub Releases, OSF, local directories). Key architectural patterns: Single build entry point: projr_build_patch/minor/major() production; projr_build_dev() development. YAML-driven configuration: project settings live _projr.yml project root. Manifest tracking: Every build records file hashes manifest.csv, linking outputs exact input versions. Remote destinations: unified dispatcher pattern (R/remote.R) routes file operations local, GitHub, OSF backends. Internal vs. exported functions: Internal helpers use . prefix (e.g., .hash_file()); exported functions use projr_ prefix (e.g., projr_build_patch()).

title: Build Process
text: projr two build types: Production builds create versioned releases: Development builds iterate without incrementing version: projr_build() wrapper accepts bump_component argument: follow three-stage architecture:
code: projr_build_patch(msg = "Fix analysis bug")   # 0.0.X projr_build_minor(msg = "Add new section")    # 0.X.0 projr_build_major(msg = "Complete rewrite")   # X.0.0 projr_build_dev() projr_build_dev(file = "analysis.qmd") projr_build(bump_component = "patch", msg = "Release") ┌─────────────┐ │  Pre-Build  │ ─── Preparation, validation, versioning └──────┬──────┘        │        ▼ ┌─────────────┐ │    Build    │ ─── Document rendering and script execution └──────┬──────┘        │        ▼ ┌─────────────┐ │ Post-Build  │ ─── Finalization, distribution, commits └─────────────┘

title: Concepts
text: article covers key ideas behind projr: single-purpose directories, versioned builds, manifests, archiving, profiles, environment variables, dependency management renv.

title: Concepts
text: projr organises projects directory one job: makes straightforward share specific parts project (e.g. just data outputs), restore new machine, understand layout glance.
code: my-project/ ├── _raw_data/          # Source data (never modified) ├── _output/            # Final outputs (figures, tables) ├── _tmp/               # Temporary/cache files ├── docs/               # Rendered documents (HTML, PDF) ├── R/                  # Source code ├── analysis.Rmd        # Analysis documents └── _projr.yml          # Configuration

title: Concepts
text: Every directory gets label describes role. label prefix determines projr treats directory: raw-* — source inputs (e.g. raw-data) cache-* — temporary storage (e.g. cache) output-* — final outputs (e.g. output) docs-* — documentation (e.g. docs) can define multiple directories prefix: Labels must end -empty (reserved internal use).
code: directories:   raw-data-public:     path: _raw_data_public   raw-data-sensitive:     path: _raw_data_sensitive   output-figures:     path: _output/figures   output-tables:     path: _output/tables

title: Concepts
text: request directory path, safe argument controls location get: safe = TRUE — versioned cache path (e.g. _tmp/projr/v0.0.1/output). Used dev builds final directories touched. safe = FALSE — actual directory (e.g. _output). Used final builds.
code: projr_path_get_dir("output") projr_path_get_dir("output", safe = TRUE)

title: Concepts
text: build assigns semantic version (major.minor.patch) project records inputs produced outputs: Major (x): breaking changes major milestones Minor (y): new features analyses Patch (z): small fixes can read set version directly:
code: v0.1.0  Initial analysis v0.1.1  Fix typo in figure v0.2.0  Add sensitivity analysis v1.0.0  Final publication version projr_version_get() projr_version_set("0.2.0")

title: Concepts
text: build types follow phases: Clear output directories (mode depends PROJR_CLEAR_OUTPUT) Run pre-build hooks Hash input files manifest Bump version (final builds ) Execute build scripts / render documents Hash output files, write manifest Commit Git (configured) Run post-build hooks Distribute remote destinations (final builds ) Bump dev version (final builds , e.g. 0.0.2 → 0.0.2-1)

title: Concepts
text: manifest CSV (manifest.csv project root) records file hashes every version. links output exact inputs produced . Query manifest see changed:
code: label,fn,version,hash raw-data,data.csv,v0.1.0,abc123... output,figure.png,v0.1.0,def456... docs,report.html,v0.1.0,ghi789... # Changes between two versions projr_manifest_changes("0.0.1", "0.0.2")  # Filter to a single label projr_manifest_changes("0.0.1", "0.0.2", label = "output")  # File history across a range of versions projr_manifest_range("0.0.1")  # Most recent change for each label projr_manifest_last_change()

title: Concepts
text: projr can archive directory contents GitHub Releases local directories build.

title: Concepts
text: Two strategies control archives organised: archive — version gets archive (preserves history) latest — build overwrites previous archive (saves space) Add remote destination R:
code: projr_yml_dest_add_github(   title = "my-release",   content = "output",   structure = "archive" )  projr_yml_dest_add_local(   title = "backup",   content = "raw-data",   path = "/mnt/shared/backups",   structure = "latest" )

title: Concepts
text: Restore full project (raw data + outputs) remotes: update single label: projr tries configured remote order (GitHub, OSF, local) uses first one requested content.
code: projr_restore_repo("owner/repo") projr_content_update(label = "raw-data") projr_content_update(label = "output", version = "0.1.0")

title: Concepts
text: profile alternative _projr.yml overrides specific settings. Profile files named _projr-<name>.yml inherit everything explicitly overridden base _projr.yml. Create activate profile: Activate via environment variable: .Renviron: Example _projr-dev.yml disables GitHub archiving Git commits:
code: _projr.yml            # Base configuration _projr-dev.yml        # Development overrides _projr-public.yml     # Public sharing overrides projr_profile_create("dev") projr_profile_get() Sys.setenv(PROJR_PROFILE = "dev") PROJR_PROFILE=dev build:   github:     enabled: false   git:     commit: false

title: Concepts
text: projr reads several environment variables. Set R, .Renviron, projr_env_set(): .Renviron: Key variables: PROJR_PROFILE — active profile name PROJR_OUTPUT_LEVEL — console verbosity (none, std, debug) PROJR_CLEAR_OUTPUT — clear output dirs (pre, post, never) PROJR_LOG_DETAILED — write detailed log files (TRUE/FALSE) PROJR_AUTO_INSTALL — auto-install missing R packages (TRUE/FALSE) GITHUB_PAT — GitHub personal access token OSF_PAT — OSF personal access token projr also supports per-project environment files loaded build time. order increasing priority: _environment — global (committed Git) _environment-<profile> — profile-specific _environment.local — local overrides (git-ignored)
code: # In R Sys.setenv(PROJR_PROFILE = "dev") Sys.setenv(PROJR_OUTPUT_LEVEL = "debug")  # Or use the helper projr_env_set(profile = "dev") PROJR_PROFILE=dev PROJR_OUTPUT_LEVEL=std

title: Concepts
text: renv locks R package versions renv.lock builds reproducible months years later. projr wraps common renv operations: Use renv long-term reproducibility matters (publications, shared projects). Skip quick exploratory work.
code: # Initialise renv for the project projr_init_renv()  # Snapshot current package versions projr_renv_update()  # Restore packages from the lockfile projr_renv_restore()

title: Concepts
text: short: organise files purpose, iterate dev builds, release versioned builds, restore anywhere single command.
code: # 1. Initialise project projr_init()  # 2. Place raw data in _raw_data/ # 3. Write analysis in .Rmd or .qmd files  # 4. Iterate with dev builds projr_build_dev() # Check outputs in _tmp/projr/v0.0.1/  # 5. First release projr_build_patch() # Outputs in _output/, archived to remotes  # 6. Keep working, then release again projr_build_minor()  # 7. Collaborator restores the project projr_restore_repo("you/your-project")

title: Design
text: article explains design decisions behind projr works way .

title: Design
text: Goal: Reduce mental burden maintaining reproducible research. projr achieves : One function remember: projr_build() everything Sensible defaults: projects work ---box Convention configuration: Standard directory structure Gradual complexity: Start simple, customise needed Anti-pattern: Complex build systems requiring extensive configuration, multiple commands, deep understanding internals. Example: Compare typical make-based workflow: projr:
code: # Traditional approach make clean make data make analysis make figures make paper make deploy-to-osf git add . git commit -m "Update" git push gh release create v0.1.0 # ... upload files manually ... projr_build() # That's it

title: Design
text: Goal: Make safe experiment without losing work. projr achieves : Dev builds: Route outputs cache, never touch _output Manifests: Always know inputs created outputs Git integration: Automatic commits preserve history Reversible versioning: Can always access previous versions via Git + archives Anti-pattern: -place modification output directories leading lost results. Example: Without projr, might: projr:
code: # Accidentally overwrite yesterday's figures render("analysis.Rmd") # Oh no, the new plot is worse! # Now you've lost the good version # Safe iteration projr_build_dev() # Outputs to _tmp/ # Check results, if bad, just run again # If good: projr_build() # Now commit to _output

title: Design
text: Goal: Automate tedious tasks whilst maintaining transparency. projr achieves : Explicit configuration: _projr.yml makes everything visible Predictable behaviour: inputs → outputs Inspectable artefacts: Manifests, build logs, Git history hidden state: configuration version-controlled files Anti-pattern: Build systems hidden state, implicit dependencies, configuration scattered across multiple locations. Example: projr’s manifest system: can inspect, version-control, audit . Compare system tracks dependencies binary database -memory cache.
code: label,path,hash,version,timestamp raw-data,_raw_data,abc123...,v0.1.0,2024-01-15

title: Design
text: Goal: Make easier reproducible . projr achieves : Automatic versioning: Every build versioned Manifests: Inputs-outputs linkage automatic renv integration: Optional package version locking Archiving: Automatic upload GitHub/OSF Restoration: One command reconstruct Anti-pattern: Reproducibility afterthought requiring manual effort. Example: Without thinking , projr users get: automatically. reproduce:
code: v0.1.0:   - code: Git SHA abc123   - data: Hash def456   - outputs: Hash ghi789   - packages: renv.lock   - archived: GitHub Release v0.1.0 projr_restore_repo("owner/repo") renv::restore() projr_build()

title: Design
text: Principle: directory exactly one purpose. Rationale: Clarity: ambiguity things go Selective sharing: Archive ’s needed Automation: Tools can act directories knowing contents Restoration: Simple mapping label path Trade-offs: ✅ Structure immediately obvious ✅ Easy share/archive specific parts ❌ directories minimal structure ❌ redundancy (e.g., separate output-figures output-tables) trade-worth : clarity automation benefits outweigh slight increase directory count. Modern file systems handle many directories efficiently.

title: Design
text: Principle: Version entire project state, individual files. Rationale: Coherence: files version X consistent Simplicity: One version number, per-file versions Traceability: Know exactly produced Restoration: Restore entire consistent state Trade-offs: ✅ Simpler mental model (one version) ✅ Guarantees consistency ❌ Version bumps even small changes ❌ Can’t mix versions different components trade-worth : Scientific outputs depend multiple inputs. Versioning whole project ensures can always reconstruct consistent state. Per-file versioning leads combinatorial explosion possible states.

title: Design
text: Principle: Project structure build behaviour _projr.yml, scattered across code. Rationale: Centralised: One place understand project configuration Readable: YAML human-readable Version-controlled: Configuration changes tracked Shareable: Easy share configuration across projects Trade-offs: ✅ Configuration explicit visible ✅ Easy diff merge configuration changes ❌ Less flexible code-based configuration ❌ YAML syntax can tricky trade-worth : research projects don’t need flexibility code-based configuration. benefits single, readable, version-controlled configuration file outweigh limitations.

title: Design
text: Principle: Separate safe iteration committed releases. Rationale: Safety: Dev builds can’t accidentally overwrite released outputs Speed: Dev builds skip versioning archiving Clarity: Explicit distinction “testing” “committing” Trade-offs: ✅ Safe experimentation ✅ Fast feedback loop ❌ Two commands remember (dev vs final) ❌ Cache directory can grow large trade-worth : safety speed benefits critical iterative research. cost remembering two commands minimal.

title: Design
text: Principle: projr works without Git, works better . Rationale: Accessibility: Beginners can use projr without learning Git Power: Advanced users get automatic Git integration Flexibility: Use Git features without learning Trade-offs: ✅ Low barrier entry ✅ Automatic Git want ❌ complex codebase (supporting paths) ❌ features require Git (versioning) trade-worth : Git powerful intimidating. making optional, projr reaches users whilst still offering Git benefits want .

title: Design
text: projr organised layers: Benefits: Modularity: layer can tested independently Extensibility: New backends (e.g., Zenodo) can added Clarity: Separation concerns
code: User-facing API (projr_build, projr_init, ...)          ↓ Configuration layer (YAML parsing, validation)          ↓ Build engine (rendering, versioning, archiving)          ↓ Backend services (Git, GitHub, OSF, file system)

title: Design
text: projr uses systematic naming: projr_* - exported functions .projr_* - Internal functions (exported) projr_build* - Build-related functions projr_init* - Initialisation functions projr_yml_* - YAML configuration functions projr_path_* - Path helper functions Benefits: Discoverability: Autocomplete groups related functions Clarity: Function purpose obvious name Namespace: public functions prefixed avoid conflicts

title: Design
text: projr uses precedence configuration: Environment variables (highest) Profile YAML (_projr-{profile}.yml) Default YAML (_projr.yml) Built-defaults (lowest) Example: Final value: _temp_output Benefits: Flexibility: Different contexts without editing files Explicitness: Clear hierarchy precedence Debuggability: Easy trace setting comes
code: # Built-in default output: _output  # Overridden in _projr.yml output: _my_output  # Overridden in _projr-dev.yml (if PROJR_PROFILE=dev) output: _dev_output  # Overridden by environment variable (if set) PROJR_OUTPUT_DIR=_temp_output

title: Design
text: Manifests use CSV simplicity compatibility: CSV? Universal: Readable tool (R, Python, Excel) Simple: complex parsing Diff-friendly: Git can show line--line changes Human-readable: Open text editor spreadsheet Alternative considered: JSON ✅ structured ❌ Less human-readable ❌ Harder diff ❌ Overkill simple tabular data
code: label,path,hash,version,timestamp raw-data,_raw_data,abc123...,v0.1.0,2024-01-15T10:30:00Z

title: Design
text: Decision: Use x.y.z versioning (major.minor.patch) Rationale: Familiar: developers know SemVer Expressive: Can communicate scale changes Tooling: Many tools understand SemVer Alternative considered: Date-based versioning (2024-01-15) ✅ Chronological ordering ❌ Doesn’t communicate significance changes ❌ Multiple versions per day require disambiguation

title: Design
text: Decision: GitHub Releases default archive destination Rationale: Ubiquity: R projects already use GitHub Free: Unlimited public releases, generous private quotas Integrated: Works existing Git workflow Accessible: Web interface downloads Alternative considered: OSF primary ✅ Designed research ✅ Better large datasets ❌ Separate account/authentication ❌ Less familiar R developers Solution: Support ; default GitHub familiarity.

title: Design
text: Decision: Default clearing _output final builds Rationale: Correctness: Ensures outputs match current code cruft: Old outputs don’t linger Idempotency: code → outputs Alternative considered: Incremental updates ✅ Faster (update changed files) ❌ Risk stale files ❌ Non-deterministic (depends previous state) Solution: Clear default; allow override via PROJR_OUTPUT_CLEAR.

what: Why route dev builds to cache?
title: Design
text: Decision: Dev builds write _tmp/projr/v<version>/ _output Rationale: Safety: Can’t accidentally overwrite released outputs Isolation: Multiple dev builds don’t conflict Cleanup: Cache can deleted without losing work Alternative considered: Use _output flag prevent overwrites ✅ Simpler mental model (one output location) ❌ Risk accidental overwrites ❌ Harder keep dev release outputs separate

title: Design
text: Decision: Use YAML configuration Rationale: Familiar: R users know YAML (R Markdown, pkgdown) Readable: Comments, quotes strings Expressive: Supports lists, nested structures Alternatives considered: TOML: - ✅ Simpler syntax - ❌ Less familiar R ecosystem - ❌ Harder nest deeply JSON: - ✅ Strict, machine-friendly - ❌ Less human-readable (quotes, comments) - ❌ Harder hand-edit

title: Design
text: design considerations future versions: 1. Incremental builds Idea: rebuild changed documents Pros: Faster builds, less re-rendering Cons: complexity, risk stale outputs Decision: Consider v2.0 careful invalidation logic 2. Dependency graphs Idea: Track outputs depend inputs Pros: Finer-grained rebuilding, better traceability Cons: Complexity, requires analysing code Decision: Interesting --scope now 3. Remote execution Idea: Build CI/cloud instead locally Pros: Reproducible environment, faster hardware Cons: Network dependency, setup complexity Decision: Possible via existing CI integrations (GitHub Actions) 4. Multi-language support Idea: Support Python, Julia, etc., just R Pros: Broader audience, use cases Cons: Different ecosystems, maintenance Decision: Focus R first; generalise later demand exists

title: Destination Send Workflow
text: run production build (projr_build_patch(), projr_build_minor(), projr_build_major()), projr can automatically send artifacts configured remote destinations. process controlled .dest_send_label() function R/dest-send-label.R, orchestrates three distinct stages: Getting Remotes - Resolve remote locations metadata Building Plan - Determine files need added, removed, updated Implementing Plan - Execute file operations update remote metadata stage builds upon previous one ensure efficient accurate synchronization local project remote destinations.

title: Destination Send Workflow
text: first stage resolves remote locations determines versions exist remote destination. Key Outputs: remote_pre - base remote location (e.g., GitHub release tag, local directory path) remote_dest_full - files uploaded (full version, empty) remote_dest_empty - Empty version variant (GitHub empty placeholders) version_comp - Version compare (NULL trusted version available) remote_comp - actual remote object used comparison Version Comparison Logic: version_comp determines remote version can trusted comparisons: NULL - trusted version available; treat remote empty (upload everything) Version string - Trusted version exists; compare local files version latest structure: - Uses current remote contents exist - Trusts manifest available valid archive structure: - Finds latest archive version matches local state - Must recent enough (local changes since version) - inspect = "file", always trusts version hashing files - inspect = "manifest", trusts manifest valid

title: Destination Send Workflow
text: second stage compares local remote state determine files need uploaded, removed, updated. Key Outputs: fn_add - Files upload remote fn_rm - Files remove remote version - Updated VERSION file content manifest - Updated manifest.csv content purge - Whether purge remote files upload ensure_remote_dest_exists - Whether create remote doesn’t exist is_remote_dest_empty - Whether remote empty operations Send Strategies: Different strategies determine local remote files compared: upload-- Upload local files, ignore remote state upload-missing - upload files don’t exist remote sync-diff - Upload new/changed files, remove deleted files sync-purge - Remove remote files, upload local files Inspection Modes: inspect parameter controls projr determines ’s remote: manifest - Use remote manifest.csv (fast, requires valid manifest) file - Download hash actual files (slower, always accurate) none - Treat remote empty (always upload everything)

title: Destination Send Workflow
text: third stage executes file operations updates remote metadata. Key Operations: Purge (purge = TRUE) - Remove existing files remote Add files - Upload files fn_add remote destination Remove files - Delete files fn_rm remote destination GitHub: manages -empty.zip placeholder files local: manages empty version directories Ensures one variant exists (full takes priority) Update metadata - Upload manifest.csv, VERSION file, optionally CHANGELOG.md Remote Variants: archive structures, projr manages two variants version: Full remote (remote_dest_full) - Contains actual files (e.g., output-v0.0.1.zip) Empty remote (remote_dest_empty) - Placeholder files exist (e.g., output-v0.0.1-empty.zip) allows projr distinguish : - “Version never uploaded” (neither variant exists) - “Version uploaded empty” (empty variant exists) - “Version uploaded files” (full variant exists) one variant exists time. files added empty remote, empty variant deleted full variant created.

title: Destination Send Workflow
text: workflow maintains trust system ensure accurate synchronization: Trusted Versions (can use manifest comparison): - Uploaded sync-diff sync-purge strategy - valid manifest.csv remote - local changes since version Untrusted Versions (must hash actual files): - Uploaded upload-upload-missing strategy - manifest.csv remote - Manifest doesn’t match local manifest version VERSION file tracks trust status asterisk: version untrusted, projr falls back hashing actual files (inspect = "file") even inspect = "manifest" configured.
code: project: 0.0.2 output: 0.0.1*  # Asterisk means untrusted raw-data: 0.0.1  # No asterisk means trusted

title: Destination Send Workflow
text: Fast Configuration (recommended default): fastest : - Archives preserve version history - -change skips unchanged content - sync-diff uploads differences - manifest avoids downloading files Simple Configuration (easier understand): simpler slower: - Always uploads files - comparison logic needed - manifest dependency
code: structure: archive send_cue: if-change send_strategy: sync-diff send_inspect: manifest structure: latest send_cue: always send_strategy: upload-all send_inspect: none

title: Directory Licenses

title: Environment Variables
text: One variable per line. Names must match [-zA-Z][-zA-Z0-9_]*. Values taken -= (leading/trailing whitespace trimmed). quotes needed — quotes become part value. Empty values (VAR=) skipped. Lines starting # comments; inline # comments also supported. put spaces around =. KEY = value won’t parse correctly.

title: Environment Variables
text: PROJR_OUTPUT_LEVEL controls console verbosity. debug shows detailed messages including remote operations. PROJR_CLEAR_OUTPUT controls output directories cleared: pre clears build, post , never skips clearing. PROJR_LOG_DETAILED controls detailed .qmd log files cache/projr/log/. Build history (builds.md) always maintained. PROJR_AUTO_INSTALL auto-installs missing R packages without prompting. unset, interactive sessions prompt user; non-interactive sessions error. Build function parameters (e.g., output_level, clear_output) override environment variables explicitly provided.
code: Sys.setenv(PROJR_OUTPUT_LEVEL = "debug") Sys.setenv(PROJR_LOG_DETAILED = "FALSE") projr_build_dev()

title: How-to guides
text: creates _projr.yml, DESCRIPTION, README.md, directories (_raw_data, _output, _tmp, docs). Git, GitHub, license, citation setup optional prompted skipped depending function used. GitHub credential prompts fail, run projr_instr_auth_github() setup instructions. Initialisation won’t overwrite existing config files.
code: library(projr)  # Interactive prompts (recommended for first-time users) projr_init_prompt()  # Sensible defaults, no prompts projr_init()  # Full setup: Git, GitHub, license, citation, renv, etc. projr_init_all() # Set license and citation separately projr_init_license(license = "MIT") projr_init_cite(title = "My Research Project", authors = "Jane Doe")

title: How-to guides
text: Use projr_build_dev() iterating code documents creating versioned release. Dev builds route outputs cache _tmp/projr/v<version>/ instead _output. Always use projr_path_get() paths resolve correctly dev final builds: Hardcoded paths like "_output/plot.png" bypass cache write directly _output dev builds.
code: projr_build_dev()  # Build specific documents only projr_build_dev("analysis.Rmd") projr_build_dev(c("methods.Rmd", "results.Rmd")) # In your R Markdown or scripts: fig_path <- projr_path_get("output", "figures", "plot.png") png(filename = fig_path) # ... plotting code ... dev.off()  # Dev build:  _tmp/projr/v0.1.0/output/figures/plot.png # Final build: _output/figures/plot.png

title: How-to guides
text: final build clears output directories, renders documents, bumps version, writes manifest, uploads configured destinations, commits Git (configured). Control output clearing PROJR_CLEAR_OUTPUT env var ("pre", "post", "never"): Git behaviour configured _projr.yml:
code: # Patch bump (0.1.0 -> 0.1.1) — projr_build() is equivalent projr_build_patch()  # Minor bump (0.1.0 -> 0.2.0) projr_build_minor()  # Major bump (0.1.0 -> 1.0.0) projr_build_major() Sys.setenv(PROJR_CLEAR_OUTPUT = "pre") projr_build() build:   git:     commit: true     push: true

title: How-to guides
text: projr supports three destination types: GitHub Releases, OSF, local directories. Configure _projr.yml: add destinations programmatically: Key parameters: content: directory archive (raw-data, output, docs, cache) cue: upload — “always”, “new” (changed), “never” strategy: “archive” (versioned) “latest” (overwrite) GitHub 2 GB limit per release asset. Use local OSF larger datasets.
code: build:   github:     raw-data:       content: [raw-data]       description: "Raw data files"       cue: "always"     output:       content: [output]       description: "Analysis outputs"    local:     path: "~/Dropbox/my-project-archive"     raw-data:       content: [raw-data]       path: "data"     output:       content: [output]       path: "outputs" projr_yml_dest_add_github(   label = "raw-data",   content = "raw-data",   description = "Raw data files" )  projr_yml_dest_add_local(   label = "raw-data",   content = "raw-data",   path = "~/archive/data" )

title: How-to guides
text: Restore specific directories: projr checks configured sources order: GitHub Releases, OSF, local archives. Ensure credentials set restoring remote sources.
code: # Clone a repo and restore archived content projr_restore_repo("owner/repo") projr_restore_repo("owner/repo", path = "~/projects/my-project")  # Restore from the current working directory (already cloned) projr_restore_repo_wd() projr_content_update(label = "raw-data") projr_content_update(label = c("raw-data", "output")) projr_content_update(label = "raw-data", version = "v0.1.0")

title: How-to guides
text: Default labels: raw-data → _raw_data cache → _tmp output → _output docs → docs Add custom labels _projr.yml: Labels must start raw, cache, output, docs. label maps single, unique path. Directories created automatically build.
code: directories:   raw-data:     path: _raw_data   raw-data-public:     path: _raw_data_public   raw-data-private:     path: _raw_data_private   cache:     path: _tmp   output:     path: _output   output-figures:     path: _output/figures   docs:     path: docs projr_path_get("raw-data-public") projr_path_get("output-figures", "plot.png")

title: How-to guides
text: Profiles let maintain different configurations different contexts (development vs production, different machines, etc.). Edit _projr-dev.yml override specific settings. Unspecified keys fall back _projr.yml. Activate profile: Remember unset PROJR_PROFILE switching back default configuration.
code: # Create a profile (creates _projr-dev.yml) projr_profile_create("dev") build:   github:     enabled: false  directories:   output:     path: _output_dev Sys.setenv(PROJR_PROFILE = "dev") projr_build()  # Check active profile projr_profile_get()  # Delete a profile projr_profile_delete("dev")

title: How-to guides
text: Build history logged cache/projr/log/{dev,output}/history/builds.md. build also creates detailed Quarto log file organised date. Control logging: Clear old logs: Logs never cleared automatically — manage manually.
code: # View build logs projr_log_view()  # List detailed log files list.files("cache/projr/log/dev/output",   recursive = TRUE, pattern = "\\.qmd$" ) # Disable detailed log files (history still maintained) Sys.setenv(PROJR_LOG_DETAILED = "FALSE")  # Re-enable Sys.setenv(PROJR_LOG_DETAILED = "TRUE") projr_log_clear() projr_log_clear(build_type = "dev") projr_log_clear(before_date = "2025-01-01")

title: How-to guides
text: manifest (manifest.csv) tracks file hashes across versions. updated final builds, dev builds. Compare two versions: Track files across range versions: Check directories last changed: Practical example — detect raw data changes since release: Version strings accept "0.0.1" "v0.0.1". query functions return 0-row data.frame results.
code: projr_manifest_changes("0.0.1", "0.0.2") projr_manifest_changes("0.0.1", "0.0.2", label = "output") projr_manifest_range("0.0.1") projr_manifest_range("0.0.1", "0.0.5", label = "raw-data") projr_manifest_last_change() projr_manifest_last_change("0.0.3") raw_changes <- projr_manifest_changes(   "0.1.0", projr_version_get(), label = "raw-data" ) if (nrow(raw_changes) > 0) {   message("Raw data has changed - outputs may need regeneration") }

title: Restoring Artifacts
code: library(projr)  # Clone and restore (most common) projr_restore_repo("owner/repo")  # Restore artifacts in an existing project projr_content_update()  # Restore only raw data projr_content_update(label = "raw-data")

title: Restoring Artifacts
text: Clones GitHub repository restores artifacts one step. function reads _projr.yml cloned repo locate configured remotes, downloads raw-* artifacts default. Cache included unless requested explicitly. Returns TRUE success.
code: projr_restore_repo(   repo,          # "owner/repo" or "repo"   path = NULL,   # clone destination (default: subdirectory named after repo)   label = NULL,  # which artifacts (default: all raw-* labels)   pos = NULL,    # "source", "dest", or both (default: both)   type = NULL,   # "github", "local", or "osf" (default: first available)   title = NULL   # remote title from _projr.yml (default: first available) ) # Clone into a subdirectory projr_restore_repo("owner/repo")  # Clone to a specific path projr_restore_repo("owner/repo", path = "~/projects/my-analysis")

title: Restoring Artifacts
text: convenience wrapper clones directly current working directory. writes files current directory, make sure right place calling .
code: projr_restore_repo_wd(   repo,   label = NULL,   pos = NULL,   type = NULL,   title = NULL ) setwd("~/projects/my-analysis") projr_restore_repo_wd("owner/repo")

title: Restoring Artifacts
text: Restores artifacts existing local project (cloning). project must manifest.csv root. manifest.csv missing, project needs least one successful projr_build_*() run first.
code: projr_content_update(   label = NULL,  # which artifacts (default: all raw-* labels)   pos = NULL,    # "source", "dest", or both   type = NULL,   # "github", "local", or "osf"   title = NULL   # remote title from _projr.yml ) # Restore everything raw projr_content_update()  # Restore raw data and cache projr_content_update(label = c("raw-data", "cache"))  # Restore from a specific remote projr_content_update(type = "local", title = "network-backup")

title: Restoring Artifacts
text: label – character vector directory labels restore. NULL (default) restores raw-* labels. Valid labels include raw-data, cache, output, docs. Prefer regenerating output/docs projr_build_dev() rather restoring . type – remote type: "github", "local", "osf". NULL (default) uses first available remote _projr.yml order. title – selects specific remote configuration multiple exist type. NULL (default) uses first available. pos – "source" source directories, "dest" build destinations, (default). Usually default fine. repo (restore_repo functions ) – GitHub repository "owner/repo" "repo" format. path (projr_restore_repo ) – clone. NULL creates subdirectory; "." clones current directory.

title: Scripts and Hooks
text: Hooks run custom R scripts specific points build process.

title: Scripts and Hooks
text: Hooks plain character vectors organized stage. Three stage keys available: pre (build), post (build), (runs stages). File paths relative project root. build.hooks ignored dev builds. dev.hooks ignored production builds. Unlike scripts, fallback .
code: build:   hooks:     pre:       - hooks/setup.R       - hooks/download-data.R     post:       - hooks/cleanup.R     both:       - hooks/log-timestamp.R  dev:   hooks:     pre: hooks/dev-setup.R

title: Scripts and Hooks
text: Hooks within stage run order listed. hook runs isolated child environment global environment, objects created one hook available subsequent hooks. share data hooks, save disk (e.g., saveRDS()). hooks run build flow: Like scripts, hook files validated build starts. missing hook file causes immediate error. hook fails runtime, build stops.
code: Production build:   1. Pre-build hooks   2. Version bump   3. Clear output directories   4. Git commit (if configured)   5. Render scripts   6. Git commit outputs (if configured)   7. Distribute to remotes   8. Post-build hooks  Development build:   1. Dev pre-build hooks   2. Clear output directories   3. Render scripts   4. Dev post-build hooks

code: projr_yml_hooks_add(path = "hooks/setup.R", stage = "pre") projr_yml_hooks_add_pre("hooks/check-auth.R") projr_yml_hooks_add_post("hooks/cleanup.R")  # Append instead of overwriting projr_yml_hooks_add("hooks/extra.R", stage = "pre", overwrite = FALSE)  # Remove all hooks projr_yml_hooks_rm_all()

title: Sending to Remotes
text: Prerequisites: Git repository connected GitHub GitHub PAT repo scope (run projr_instr_auth_github() setup) Token stored _environment.local (git-ignored): GITHUB_PAT=ghp_... YAML equivalent: Parameters: title – release name. Use @version automatic versioning (e.g. "raw-data-v0.1.0"). Spaces become hyphens. Must unique per repository. content – directory label archive (raw-data, cache, output, docs, code). Note: GitHub 2 GB limit per release asset. larger datasets, use local remotes split across multiple releases.
code: # Add a GitHub release for raw data projr_yml_dest_add_github(   title = "raw-data-@version",   content = "raw-data" )  # Add a GitHub release for outputs projr_yml_dest_add_github(   title = "output-@version",   content = "output" ) build:   github:     raw-data-release:       title: "raw-data-@version"       content: [raw-data]     output-release:       title: "output-@version"       content: [output]

text: authentication required. Parent directories must exist first build. Use absolute paths clarity; relative paths resolve project root. code: # Archive to local directory projr_yml_dest_add_local(   title = "local-backup",   content = "output",   path = "~/project-archive/output" )  # Archive to network drive projr_yml_dest_add_local(   title = "network-backup",   content = "raw-data",   path = "/mnt/shared/projects/my-project/data" ) build:   local:     local-backup:       title: "local-backup"       content: [output]       path: "~/project-archive/output"

title: Sending to Remotes
text: Remotes activate automatically production builds: Build steps remotes configured: Clear output directories, run pre-build hooks, hash input files Bump version Render documents scripts Hash output files, update manifest Git commit (configured) Upload remotes Run post-build hooks Check validate configuration: remotes aren’t uploading, verify running production build (projr_build_dev()), send_cue "never", authentication configured GitHub destinations.
code: projr_build_dev()   # does NOT upload to remotes projr_build_patch() # uploads (0.1.0 -> 0.1.1) projr_build_minor() # uploads (0.1.0 -> 0.2.0) projr_build_major() # uploads (0.1.0 -> 1.0.0) projr_yml_get()$build$github projr_yml_get()$build$local projr_yml_check()

title: Sending to Remotes
text: Use @version GitHub release titles; use descriptive prefixes like "raw-data-@version". Archive raw-data separately output. Start defaults: structure = "archive", send_strategy = "sync-diff", send_cue = "-change", send_inspect = "manifest". Use send_cue = "-change" large, rarely-changing datasets. local remotes, prefer absolute paths (~/archive/, /mnt/shared/). Store tokens _environment.local – never commit secrets. Test configuration projr_build_dev() first (uploads). slow uploads, switch "sync-diff" + "manifest" inspection.

title: Sending to Remotes
text: Recommended defaults:
code: # Minimal GitHub remote projr_yml_dest_add_github(   title = "output-@version",   content = "output" )  # Minimal local remote projr_yml_dest_add_local(   title = "output-backup",   content = "output",   path = "~/archive/output" )  # Run production build projr_build_patch()

title: projr Package Development Guidelines
text: Make minimal, surgical changes Maintain backward compatibility Follow existing patterns Add tests new functionality/bug fixes Never leave trailing whitespace Always add blank line headings bullets committing: - Run devtools::document(), devtools::test() (LITE mode), styler::style_pkg() - Update _pkgdown.yml adding/removing exported functions - Verify pkgdown::check_pkgdown() - Ensure files end single newline, trailing whitespace - Update copilot instructions needed Package structure: - R/ - Source (. prefix internal, projr_ exported) - tests/testthat/ - Tests (use helper-*.R functions) - man/ - Auto-generated docs (edit) - _projr.yml - Project configuration

title: projr Package Development Guidelines
text: Input validation: - Use .assert_*() functions R/check.R - Validate user inputs internal calls - Provide clear error messages Logging: - Log files auto-created builds (projr_build_*) - Outside builds: Use .log_build_init() enable logging - .cli_*() functions write log: .cli_info(), .cli_success(), .cli_debug(), .cli_step() - Commit useful .cli_debug() calls; avoid logging secrets large output Interactive debugging: - Use debugonce() function-level debugging - Guard browser() calls: (interactive()) browser() - Use traceback(), rlang::last_error(), rlang::last_trace() diagnostics - Remove/guard interactive statements committing Test debugging: - General runs: Use LITE mode (.test_set_lite()) - Specific failures: Turn LITE (.test_unset_lite()), use SELECT (.test_set_select()), comment skip_if(.is_test_select()) target tests - See testing.instructions.md details

title: projr Package Development Guidelines
text: Version management: major.minor.patch-dev format; projr_version_get(), projr_version_set() Build system: Production (projr_build_patch/minor/major()), dev (projr_build_dev()); env vars: PROJR_OUTPUT_LEVEL, PROJR_CLEAR_OUTPUT, PROJR_LOG_DETAILED Manifest: Tracks file hashes manifest.csv; query: projr_manifest_changes(), projr_manifest_range(), projr_manifest_last_change() Git: Auto-commits/pushes via build.git settings; uses Git CLI gert Remotes: Local, GitHub, OSF destinations; restore: projr_content_update(), projr_restore_repo() Directory licenses: Per-directory LICENSE files; YAML (auto-regenerated) manual (projr_license_create_manual()); templates: CC-, CC0, Apache-2.0, MIT, Proprietary

title: projr Package Development Guidelines
text: GitHub: Set GITHUB_PAT; use .auth_check_github() gh::/gitcreds:: calls; see projr_instr_auth_github() OSF: Set OSF_PAT; use .auth_check_osf() osfr:: calls; see projr_instr_auth_osf()

title: projr Package Development Guidelines
text: Build: PROJR_OUTPUT_LEVEL (none/std/debug), PROJR_CLEAR_OUTPUT (pre/post/never), PROJR_LOG_DETAILED (TRUE/FALSE) Testing: R_PKG_TEST_LITE (skip comprehensive), R_PKG_TEST_CRAN (skip slow), R_PKG_TEST_SELECT (targeted) Files: _environment.local (git-ignored, highest), _environment-<profile> (profile), _environment (global, lowest)

title: projr Package Development Guidelines
text: Safe vs Unsafe directories: - Safe (safe = TRUE): Cache build dir (e.g., _tmp/projr/v0.0.1/output) - Unsafe (safe = FALSE): Actual dir (e.g., _output) Test modes: - LITE: Core tests (~364, ~2.5 min) - development - FULL: tests (~452, ~5+ min) - releases - CRAN: Minimal (<2 min) - submission Directory labels: raw-data, cache, output, docs, project, code, data (case-insensitive, ignore hyphens/underscores) Directory licensing: - YAML: Managed _projr.yml, auto-regenerated (good outputs) - Manual: projr_license_create_manual(), never overwritten (good raw data) - coexist; YAML takes precedence

title: projr Package Development Guidelines
text: Follow GitHub best practices: - Keep concise (250 lines ideal, max 1000) - Use clear structure: headings, bullets, sections - direct: short, imperative rules - Show examples: code samples (correct/incorrect) - external links (copy info instead) - vague language (“accurate”, etc.) - Use applyTo frontmatter topic files - Review regularly See .github/instructions/README.md details.

what: CRAN Compliance: Removed Automatic Package Installation
title: projr 0.0.8.9002
text: Removed automatic package installation package code comply CRAN policy prohibits writing user filespace checks. Pre-build package validation: Builds now check required packages (build engines, remote destinations) starting build process. packages missing, get clear error copy-paste installation command missing packages . Improved error messages: running interactively package needed installed, projr provides clear, actionable error messages exact installation commands. automatic installation occurs ensure CRAN compliance. .dep_install_only() .renv_lockfile_read() longer automatically install missing packages. Instead, throw clear, informative errors installation instructions required packages missing. Users must now manually install required optional dependencies using features depend . error messages provide exact installation commands. change affects functions previously auto-installed packages like: Build engines (quarto, rmarkdown, bookdown) Remote destinations (piggyback GitHub, osfr OSF) GitHub operations (requires gh package) Git operations test mode (requires gert package) License creation (requires usethis package) Documentation generation (requires roxygen2 package) optional features renv projects: Use renv::install() install dependencies non-renv projects: Use install.packages() remotes::install_github() shown error messages
